facts.text: Bascom was incorporated in 1901 and named after the Bascom family, early settlers who built sawmills, cotton gins, and turpentine operations.
Bascom is the birthplace of Academy Award‑winning actress Faye Dunaway, born there in 1941 before starring in Bonnie and Clyde and Network.
Bascom’s historic school building now serves as an event space and small museum preserving the town’s heritage.
Bascom Community Park hosts local events and offers river access at Neal’s Landing, a popular gathering spot.
The region’s high humidity, often above 70%, brings frequent afternoon thunderstorms that cool the heat but can bring heavy downpours.
Bascom sits just 5 miles south of the Alabama border in the Florida Panhandle, 14 miles northeast of Marianna, the county seat.
